title: Bank of America Financial Summary From 2025 Form 10-K
text: Bank of America's 2025 Form 10-K reported total revenue, net of interest expense, of $113.1 billion, compared with $105.9 billion in 2024. Net interest income was $60.1 billion, while noninterest income was $53.0 billion. Net income was $30.5 billion, or $3.81 per diluted share, compared with $27.0 billion, or $3.19 per diluted share, in 2024. At December 31, 2025, total assets were about $3.4 trillion, up $150.4 billion from the prior year, mainly due to higher loans and leases, trading account assets, and securities financing activity. Deposits were $2.02 trillion, up from $1.97 trillion at year-end 2024, and loans and leases increased by $89.9 billion. The company also repurchased $21.4 billion of common stock and paid $8.1 billion of common dividends during 2025. Main risks include credit quality, interest rates, regulation, cybersecurity, geopolitical uncertainty, and macroeconomic pressure.
